name: Basic Work Stress - Marketing Deadline

persona.description: A 30-year-old marketing professional at a mid-size tech company. They have a major product launch deadline in 3 days and feel overwhelmed by the workload. They've been sleeping poorly, snapping at coworkers, and skipping meals. They have some familiarity with therapy concepts from a podcast but have never done formal CBT. Generally articulate and willing to engage. They tend to intellectualize rather than sit with emotions.

openingMessages: I've been so stressed about this product launch at work. The deadline is in 3 days and I feel like I'm drowning. I can't sleep, I snapped at my colleague today, and I just feel like everything is falling apart.
userGoals: Identify the specific thoughts driving the stress (e.g., 'If this launch fails, I'll get fired')
Recognize the connection between thoughts, physical symptoms, and behaviors
Map the situation onto the mandala with events, thoughts, and behaviors in correct cells
Feel heard and validated before diving into analysis
Leave with at least one concrete coping strategy for the next 3 days
expectedBehaviors: Validate the stress before exploring causes ('That sounds really overwhelming')
Ask one open-ended question at a time to unpack the situation
Help identify automatic thoughts about the deadline and consequences
Map events to past-events cell, thoughts/emotions to past-thoughts-emotions cell
Gently introduce the thought-feeling-behavior connection when natural
Suggest a small, actionable step rather than a complete overhaul
antiPatterns: Jumping straight to advice ('You should make a to-do list')
Asking multiple questions in one message
Minimizing the stress ('It's just a deadline, it'll pass')
Giving prescriptive sleep or diet advice
Filling multiple mandala cells at once before exploring each
